server_like.h(3) Library Functions Manual server_like.h(3)

NAME

server_like.h - Shared initialization, networking, and teardown for server-like modes.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct session_server_like_config

Typedefs


typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_server_like_init_fn) (void *user_data)
typedef void(* session_server_like_interrupt_fn) (int sig)
typedef void(* session_server_like_cleanup_fn) (void *user_data)
typedef void(* session_server_like_status_fn) (void *user_data, ui_status_t *out_status)
typedef struct session_server_like_config session_server_like_config_t

Functions


asciichat_error_t session_server_like_run (const session_server_like_config_t *config)
tcp_server_t * session_server_like_get_tcp_server (void)
asciichat_mdns_t * session_server_like_get_mdns_ctx (void)
nat_upnp_context_t * session_server_like_get_upnp_ctx (void)
websocket_server_t * session_server_like_get_websocket_server (void)
asciichat_error_t session_server_like_mdns_advertise (const char *name, const char *type, uint16_t port, const char **txt, size_t txt_count)
asciichat_error_t session_server_like_handshake (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, acip_transport_t *transport)
bool session_server_like_shutdown_requested (void)

Detailed Description

Shared initialization, networking, and teardown for server-like modes.

Provides a unified lifecycle for modes that act as servers (accepting client connections). Both the main ascii-chat server and the discovery-service (ACDS) use this layer, analogous to how client_like serves mirror/client/discovery.

Modes Supported

  • Server mode: TCP/WebSocket server with audio mixing and H.265 encoding
  • Discovery-Service mode: ACDS session discovery and WebRTC signaling

Shared Responsibilities

This layer owns and manages:

  • TCP server lifecycle (init, accept loop, destroy)
  • WebSocket server lifecycle (init, event loop thread, destroy)
  • mDNS context and service advertisement
  • UPnP port mapping
  • Per-client crypto handshake (single shared implementation)
  • Signal handler registration (SIGINT, SIGTERM, SIGPIPE) with persistent shutdown flag
  • Keepawake system (platform sleep prevention)
  • Status screen thread with keyboard input and grep mode
  • File descriptor limit raising
  • Proper cleanup ordering with partial-init safety

Mode-Specific Responsibilities

Mode files provide callbacks for:

•
init_fn: Mode-specific setup (crypto keys, rate limiter, mixer, DB, etc.)
•
Called AFTER network infrastructure is ready but BEFORE WebSocket thread starts
•
Can detect early shutdown via session_server_like_shutdown_requested()
•
Failure triggers clean shutdown (cleanup still runs)
•
interrupt_fn(int sig): Async-signal-safe shutdown (close sockets, set exit flag)
•
Called from signal handler (SIGINT/SIGTERM)
•
Must use only async-signal-safe operations
•
Receives signal number as parameter
•
cleanup_fn: Mode-specific teardown (clients, threads, resources)
•
Called after WebSocket event loop stops
•
TCP registry still available for iteration
•
Only called if config provided (safe against NULL)
•
status_fn: Optional status screen data population
•
Called at GET_OPTION(fps) Hz by dedicated status thread
•
Receives pre-zeroed ui_status_t struct to populate
•
NULL = no status screen even if --status-screen is set

Memory and Lifecycle

server_like owns TCP server, WebSocket server, mDNS context, and UPnP context. Modes access these via accessors (e.g., session_server_like_get_tcp_server()). Mode-specific state (clients, mixer, DB) is owned by the mode.

CRITICAL SAFETY GUARANTEES:

  • Cleanup operations only destroy resources that were successfully initialized
  • Mode init failures do not cause undefined behavior during cleanup
  • Early SIGINT/SIGTERM during init is preserved and detectable
  • WebSocket callbacks do not run before mode init completes
  • Signal handler type is correct (void(*)(int)) with no casts

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

April 2026 (Refactored)

Definition in file server_like.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef void(* session_server_like_cleanup_fn) (void *user_data)

Mode-specific teardown callback.

Called after WebSocket server is stopped but before WebSocket/TCP servers are destroyed. The TCP client registry is still available for iteration (e.g., to disconnect all clients gracefully).

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer from config.cleanup_user_data

Definition at line 120 of file server_like.h.

typedef struct session_server_like_config session_server_like_config_t

Configuration for session_server_like_run().

Network settings (port, address, WebSocket port, TLS paths) are read from GET_OPTION() automatically. Callers provide callbacks, handler functions, and feature toggles.

typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_server_like_init_fn) (void *user_data)

Mode-specific initialization callback.

Called once after shared infrastructure is up (TCP server bound, WebSocket started, mDNS/UPnP configured). Should set up mode-specific state: crypto keys, rate limiter, mixer, database, etc.

The TCP server is accessible via session_server_like_get_tcp_server().

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer from config.init_user_data

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK, or error code to abort (cleanup still runs)

Definition at line 94 of file server_like.h.

typedef void(* session_server_like_interrupt_fn) (int sig)

Async-signal-safe interrupt callback.

Called from signal handler context (SIGINT / SIGTERM) with the signal number. Must only use async-signal-safe operations. Responsible for:

1.
Setting the mode's shutdown flag (atomic)
2.
Closing TCP listen sockets to unblock accept loop
3.
Optionally stopping WebSocket server

Use session_server_like_get_tcp_server() to access listen sockets.

Parameters

sig Signal number (SIGINT=2, SIGTERM=15)

Definition at line 109 of file server_like.h.

typedef void(* session_server_like_status_fn) (void *user_data, ui_status_t *out_status)

Status screen data callback.

Called at GET_OPTION(fps) Hz by the status screen thread. Should fill *out_status with current state for ui_status_display(). If NULL in config, the status screen is not shown even if --status-screen is set.

The callback receives a zeroed ui_status_t and must populate ALL fields it wants displayed.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer from config.status_user_data
out_status Output struct to populate (pre-zeroed by caller)

Definition at line 135 of file server_like.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_mdns_t * session_server_like_get_mdns_ctx (void )

Get the mDNS context owned by server_like.

READINESS GUARANTEE: Available from step 6 onward (during init_fn and after). Use for deferred advertisement when service_name is not known at config time. NULL if mDNS is disabled or initialization failed (non-fatal).

Returns

Pointer to mDNS context, or NULL if not initialized or disabled

Definition at line 266 of file server_like.c.

266                                                          {
267   return g_mdns_ctx;
268 }








tcp_server_t * session_server_like_get_tcp_server (void
  )


Get the TCP server instance owned by server_like.




READINESS GUARANTEE: Available from step 4 onward (during init_fn,
    interrupt_fn, cleanup_fn). Modes use this for client registry operations and
    to close listen sockets in interrupt_fn. The TCP server is guaranteed to be
    valid during init_fn and in interrupt_fn (async-signal context).




Returns


Pointer to TCP server (never NULL after step 4), or NULL
  if called before TCP init


Definition at line 262 of file server_like.c.


262                                                        {
263   return g_tcp_server_initialized ? &g_tcp_server : NULL;
264 }






Referenced by acds_server_init().






nat_upnp_context_t * session_server_like_get_upnp_ctx
  (void )


Get the UPnP context owned by server_like.




READINESS GUARANTEE: Available from step 5 onward (during init_fn
    and after). Use for querying the public address. NULL if port mapping failed
    or was disabled. Failure to map UPnP is non-fatal; WebRTC fallback will be
    used.




Returns


Pointer to UPnP context, or NULL if not initialized or
  disabled


Definition at line 270 of file server_like.c.


270                                                            {
271   return g_upnp_ctx;
272 }










websocket_server_t *
  session_server_like_get_websocket_server (void )


Get the WebSocket server instance owned by server_like.




READINESS GUARANTEE: WebSocket object is constructed at step 7,
    but the event loop thread does NOT start until after mode init succeeds
    (step 9). Callbacks will not run until after init_fn returns successfully.
    This prevents client connections before mode state is ready.




Available for configuration during init_fn, and for state queries
    after init_fn succeeds. The WebSocket object is valid for the lifetime of
    the session_server_like_run() call.




Returns


Pointer to WebSocket server, or NULL if not initialized
  or disabled


Definition at line 274 of file server_like.c.


274                                                                    {
275   return g_websocket_thread_started ? &g_websocket_server : NULL;
276 }










asciichat_error_t session_server_like_handshake
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, acip_transport_t *
  transport)


Perform the server-side crypto handshake on a transport.




Runs the full handshake sequence: receive PROTOCOL_VERSION, send
    parameters, key exchange, auth challenge, and completion. Both server and
    discovery-service modes use this single implementation.




The caller must initialize the handshake context
    (crypto_handshake_init or crypto_handshake_init_with_password) and configure
    keys/whitelist on it before calling this function.




Parameters


ctx Initialized handshake context with keys/auth
  configured


transport ACIP transport for the client connection


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on handshake
  failure


Definition at line 323 of file server_like.c.


323                                                                                                               {
324   if (!ctx || !transport) {
325     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "handshake context or transport is NULL");
326   }
327 
328   asciichat_error_t result;
329 
330   // Step 0: Receive PROTOCOL_VERSION from client
331   {
332     packet_type_t pkt_type;
333     void *pkt_data = NULL;
334     size_t pkt_len = 0;
335     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
336 
337     result = packet_receive_via_transport(transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
338     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
339       log_warn("Failed to receive PROTOCOL_VERSION from client");
340       return result;
341     }
342 
343     if (pkt_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
344       log_warn("Expected PROTOCOL_VERSION, got packet type %u", pkt_type);
345       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
346       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Expected PROTOCOL_VERSION packet");
347     }
348 
349     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
350     log_debug("Received PROTOCOL_VERSION from client");
351   }
352 
353   // Step 1: Send crypto parameters
354   result = crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters(ctx, transport);
355   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
356     log_warn("Crypto parameters send failed");
357     return result;
358   }
359 
360   // Step 2: Key exchange (send server key)
361   result = crypto_handshake_server_start(ctx, transport);
362   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
363     log_warn("Crypto handshake start failed");
364     return result;
365   }
366 
367   // Step 3: Receive client key exchange response, send auth challenge
368   {
369     packet_type_t pkt_type;
370     void *pkt_data = NULL;
371     size_t pkt_len = 0;
372     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
373 
374     result = packet_receive_via_transport(transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
375     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
376       log_warn("Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE");
377       return result;
378     }
379 
380     result = crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(ctx, transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
381     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
382 
383     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
384       log_warn("Crypto auth challenge failed");
385       return result;
386     }
387   }
388 
389   // Step 4: Complete handshake (skip if already complete from auth challenge)
390   if (ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
391     packet_type_t pkt_type;
392     void *pkt_data = NULL;
393     size_t pkt_len = 0;
394     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
395 
396     result = packet_receive_via_transport(transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
397     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
398       log_warn("Failed to receive AUTH_RESPONSE");
399       return result;
400     }
401 
402     result = crypto_handshake_server_complete(ctx, transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
403     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
404 
405     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
406       log_warn("Crypto handshake completion failed");
407       return result;
408     }
409   }
410 
411   log_info("Crypto handshake complete");
412   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
413 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, buffer_pool_free(),
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(),
    crypto_handshake_server_complete(),
    crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters(),
    crypto_handshake_server_start(), ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, log_debug, log_info,
    log_warn, packet_receive_via_transport(),
    PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION, SET_ERRNO, and
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state.


Referenced by acds_client_handler(), and
    acds_websocket_client_handler().






asciichat_error_t session_server_like_mdns_advertise
  (const char * name, const char * type, uint16_t port, const char **
  txt, size_t txt_count)


Advertise or re-advertise an mDNS service.




Convenience wrapper for modes that need deferred or updated
    advertisement (e.g., server mode advertises after ACDS registration provides
    session string).




Parameters


name Service instance name


type Service type (e.g., "_ascii-chat._tcp")


port Service port


txt Optional TXT records (NULL-terminated array), or NULL


txt_count Number of TXT records


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error if mDNS is not
  initialized


Definition at line 282 of file server_like.c.


283                                                                                          {
284   if (!g_mdns_ctx) {
285     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "mDNS context not initialized");
286   }
287 
288   char hostname[256] = {0};
289   gethostname(hostname, sizeof(hostname) - 1);
290 
291   asciichat_mdns_service_t service = {
292       .name = name,
293       .type = type,
294       .host = hostname,
295       .port = port,
296       .txt_records = txt,
297       .txt_count = txt_count,
298   };
299 
300   asciichat_error_t result = asciichat_mdns_advertise(g_mdns_ctx, &service);
301   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
302     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to advertise mDNS service");
303     log_warn("mDNS advertising failed - LAN discovery disabled");
304   } else {
305     log_info("mDNS: Service advertised as '%s' type '%s' (port=%d)", name, type, port);
306   }
307 
308   return result;
309 }






References asciichat_mdns_advertise(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_info,
    log_warn, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by session_server_like_run().






asciichat_error_t session_server_like_run (const
  session_server_like_config_t * config)


Run a server-like mode with unified lifecycle management.




Orchestrates the complete lifecycle with proper initialization
    ordering to ensure mode-specific state is ready before external clients can
    connect.








Initialization
  Sequence



  
1.

  
Keepawake validation and setup

  
2.

  
File descriptor limit raising (if configured)

  
3.

  
Signal handler registration (SIGINT, SIGTERM, SIGPIPE) with shutdown
      persistence

  
4.

  
TCP server init (binds to GET_OPTION port/address)

  
5.

  
UPnP port mapping (if enabled)

  
6.

  
mDNS context init and optional immediate advertisement

  
7.

  
WebSocket server construction (object created, event loop thread
    deferred)

  
8.

  
Mode-specific init_fn() - mode initializes its state (crypto keys, rate
      limiter, DB, etc.)

  
9.

  
WebSocket event loop thread start (only if mode init succeeds)

  
10.

  
Status screen and keyboard threads (if status_fn provided)

  
11.

  
TCP accept loop (blocks until shutdown)




CRITICAL ORDERING NOTES:


    
  
  • WebSocket event loop thread ONLY starts after mode init succeeds (step 9,
      not step 7)
    • 
  
  • This prevents clients from arriving before mode state is ready
    • 
  
  • If SIGINT/SIGTERM arrives during init, subsequent steps are skipped
    • 
  
  • Mode-specific init can check
      session_server_like_shutdown_requested() for early shutdowns
    • 







Cleanup
  (always runs, in order, guarded against partial init)



  
12.

  
Join status screen and keyboard threads

  
13.

  
Stop WebSocket server (only if thread was started, atomic stop, cancel,
      join with 500ms timeout)

  
14.

  
Mode-specific cleanup_fn() (TCP registry still available, guarded by mode
      state flags)

  
15.

  
Destroy WebSocket server (only if successfully initialized)

  
16.

  
Destroy TCP server

  
17.

  
Close UPnP port mapping

  
18.

  
Destroy mDNS context

  
19.

  
Disable keepawake




SAFETY GUARANTEES:


    
  
  • Cleanup operations only touch resources that were successfully
    initialized
    • 
  
  • Signal handler safely sets shutdown flag during early initialization
    • 
  
  • Modes can detect early shutdown via
      session_server_like_shutdown_requested()
    • 
  
  • Partial initialization failures do not trigger undefined behavior in
      cleanup
    • 



Parameters


Config Module Mode configuration
  (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, or first error from init or
  accept loop


Definition at line 419 of file server_like.c.


419                                                                                       {
420   if (!config) {
421     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config is NULL");
422   }
423   if (!config->init_fn) {
424     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "init_fn is NULL");
425   }
426   if (!config->interrupt_fn) {
427     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "interrupt_fn is NULL");
428   }
429   if (!config->tcp_handler) {
430     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "tcp_handler is NULL");
431   }
432 
433   g_config = config;
434   asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
435 
436   /* === 1. Keepawake === */
437 
438   bool keepawake_enabled = GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake);
439   bool keepawake_disabled = GET_OPTION(disable_keepawake);
440   if (keepawake_enabled && keepawake_disabled) {
441     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Cannot specify both --keepawake and --no-keepawake");
442   }
443   if (keepawake_enabled) {
444     platform_enable_keepawake();
445   }
446 
447   /* === 2. File descriptor limit === */
448 
449   if (config->raise_fd_limit) {
450     platform_raise_fd_limit(config->fd_limit_target);
451   }
452 
453   /* === 3. Signal handlers === */
454 
455   atomic_store_u64(&g_shutdown_requested, 0);
456   g_interrupt_fn = config->interrupt_fn;
457   platform_signal(SIGINT, server_like_signal_handler);
458   platform_signal(SIGTERM, server_like_signal_handler);
459 #ifndef _WIN32
460   platform_signal(SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN);
461 #endif
462 
463   /* === 4. TCP server init === */
464 
465   int port = GET_OPTION(port);
466   const char *address = GET_OPTION(address);
467   const char *address6 = GET_OPTION(address6);
468 
469   bool ipv4_has_value = (address && strlen(address) > 0);
470   bool ipv6_has_value = (address6 && strlen(address6) > 0);
471 
472   tcp_server_config_t tcp_config = {
473       .port = port,
474       .ipv4_address = ipv4_has_value ? address : NULL,
475       .ipv6_address = ipv6_has_value ? address6 : NULL,
476       .bind_ipv4 = ipv4_has_value || !ipv6_has_value,
477       .bind_ipv6 = ipv6_has_value || !ipv4_has_value,
478       .accept_timeout_sec = 1,
479       .client_handler = config->tcp_handler,
480       .user_data = config->tcp_user_data,
481       .status_update_fn = NULL,
482       .status_update_data = NULL,
483   };
484 
485   result = tcp_server_init(&g_tcp_server, &tcp_config);
486   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
487     log_error("Failed to initialize TCP server on port %d", port);
488     goto cleanup;
489   }
490   g_tcp_server_initialized = true;
491   log_info("TCP server initialized on port %d", port);
492 
493   /* === 5. UPnP port mapping === */
494 
495   if (config->upnp.enabled && GET_OPTION(enable_upnp)) {
496     asciichat_error_t upnp_result = nat_upnp_open(port, config->upnp.description, &g_upnp_ctx);
497 
498     if (upnp_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && g_upnp_ctx) {
499       char public_addr[22];
500       if (nat_upnp_get_address(g_upnp_ctx, public_addr, sizeof(public_addr)) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
501         char msg[256];
502         safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Public endpoint: %s (direct TCP)", public_addr);
503         log_console(LOG_INFO, msg);
504         log_info("UPnP: Port mapping successful, public endpoint: %s", public_addr);
505       }
506     } else {
507       log_info("UPnP: Port mapping unavailable or failed - will use WebRTC fallback");
508     }
509   }
510 
511   /* === 6. mDNS === */
512 
513   if (config->mdns.enabled) {
514     g_mdns_ctx = asciichat_mdns_init();
515     if (!g_mdns_ctx) {
516       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize mDNS (non-fatal, LAN discovery disabled)");
517       log_warn("mDNS disabled - LAN discovery will not be available");
518     } else if (config->mdns.service_name) {
519       // Immediate advertisement if service_name is provided
520       session_server_like_mdns_advertise(config->mdns.service_name, config->mdns.service_type, port, NULL, 0);
521     }
522   }
523 
524   /* === 7. WebSocket server construction (thread deferred until after mode init) === */
525 
526   if (config->websocket.enabled) {
527     websocket_server_config_t ws_config = {
528         .port = GET_OPTION(websocket_port),
529         .client_handler = config->websocket.handler,
530         .user_data = config->websocket.user_data,
531         .tls_cert_path = GET_OPTION(websocket_tls_cert),
532         .tls_key_path = GET_OPTION(websocket_tls_key),
533     };
534 
535     memset(&g_websocket_server, 0, sizeof(g_websocket_server));
536     asciichat_error_t ws_result = websocket_server_init(&g_websocket_server, &ws_config);
537     if (ws_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
538       log_warn("Failed to initialize WebSocket server - browser clients will not be supported");
539     } else {
540       g_websocket_initialized = true;
541       log_info("WebSocket server initialized on port %d (event loop deferred until after mode init)",
542                GET_OPTION(websocket_port));
543     }
544   }
545 
546   /* === 8. Mode-specific init === */
547 
548   result = config->init_fn(config->init_user_data);
549   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
550     log_error("Mode initialization failed");
551     goto cleanup;
552   }
553 
554   /* === 8b. WebSocket event loop thread start (after mode init succeeds) === */
555 
556   if (g_websocket_initialized && !atomic_load_u64(&g_shutdown_requested)) {
557     if (asciichat_thread_create(&g_websocket_thread, "websocket_event_loop", websocket_server_thread_wrapper,
558                                 &g_websocket_server) == 0) {
559       g_websocket_thread_started = true;
560       log_info("WebSocket event loop thread started");
561     } else {
562       log_error("Failed to start WebSocket event loop thread");
563     }
564   }
565 
566   /* === 9. Status screen === */
567 
568   if (config->status_fn) {
569     bool status_opt = GET_OPTION(status_screen);
570     bool status_explicit = GET_OPTION(status_screen_explicitly_set);
571     if ((status_opt && terminal_is_interactive()) || (status_explicit && status_opt)) {
572       atomic_store_bool(&g_status_should_exit, false);
573       ui_status_log_init();
574 
575       // Store callback and user data for status thread (avoids reliance on g_config lifetime)
576       g_status_fn = config->status_fn;
577       g_status_user_data = config->status_user_data;
578 
579       if (asciichat_thread_create(&g_status_screen_thread, "status_screen", status_screen_thread_func, NULL) == 0) {
580         g_status_screen_thread_started = true;
581         log_info("Status screen thread started");
582       } else {
583         log_error("Failed to start status screen thread");
584         ui_status_log_destroy();
585         g_status_fn = NULL;
586         g_status_user_data = NULL;
587       }
588     }
589   }
590 
591   /* === 10. TCP accept loop (blocks) === */
592 
593   log_info("Server accepting connections on port %d", port);
594   result = tcp_server_run(&g_tcp_server);
595   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
596     log_error("TCP server exited with error");
597   }
598 
599   /* === CLEANUP === */
600 
601 cleanup:
602   g_config = NULL;
603 
604   /* 11. Stop status screen */
605   if (g_status_screen_thread_started) {
606     atomic_store_bool(&g_status_should_exit, true);
607     int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_status_screen_thread, NULL, 2000 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
608     if (join_result != 0) {
609       log_warn("Status screen thread did not exit within 2s");
610     }
611     g_status_screen_thread_started = false;
612     g_status_fn = NULL;
613     g_status_user_data = NULL;
614     ui_status_log_destroy();
615   }
616 
617   /* 12. Stop WebSocket server (only if thread was started, i.e., init succeeded) */
618   if (g_websocket_thread_started) {
619     atomic_store_bool(&g_websocket_server.running, false);
620     websocket_server_cancel_service(&g_websocket_server);
621     int ws_join = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_websocket_thread, NULL, 500 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
622     if (ws_join != 0) {
623       log_warn("WebSocket event loop thread did not exit within 500ms");
624     }
625     g_websocket_thread_started = false;
626   }
627 
628   /* 13. Mode-specific cleanup (only call if config provided) */
629   if (config && config->cleanup_fn) {
630     config->cleanup_fn(config->cleanup_user_data);
631   }
632 
633   /* 14. Destroy WebSocket server (only if successfully initialized) */
634   if (g_websocket_initialized) {
635     websocket_server_destroy(&g_websocket_server);
636     g_websocket_initialized = false;
637     log_debug("WebSocket server destroyed");
638   }
639 
640   /* 15. Destroy TCP server */
641   if (g_tcp_server_initialized) {
642     tcp_server_destroy(&g_tcp_server);
643     g_tcp_server_initialized = false;
644     log_debug("TCP server destroyed");
645   }
646 
647   /* 16. UPnP cleanup */
648   if (g_upnp_ctx) {
649     nat_upnp_close(&g_upnp_ctx);
650     log_debug("UPnP port mapping closed");
651   }
652 
653   /* 17. mDNS cleanup */
654   if (g_mdns_ctx) {
655     asciichat_mdns_destroy(g_mdns_ctx);
656     g_mdns_ctx = NULL;
657     log_debug("mDNS context destroyed");
658   }
659 
660   /* 18. Keepawake */
661   if (keepawake_enabled) {
662     platform_disable_keepawake();
663   }
664 
665   return result;
666 }






References asciichat_mdns_destroy(),
    asciichat_mdns_init(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_thread_create, asciichat_thread_join_timeout(),
    atomic_load_u64(), atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_u64(), session_server_like_config::cleanup_fn,
    session_server_like_config::cleanup_user_data,
    session_server_like_config::description,
    session_server_like_config::enabled, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    session_server_like_config::fd_limit_target, GET_OPTION,
    session_server_like_config::handler,
    session_server_like_config::init_fn,
    session_server_like_config::init_user_data,
    session_server_like_config::interrupt_fn, log_console,
    log_debug, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_error,
    LOG_INFO, log_info, log_warn,
    session_server_like_config::mdns, nat_upnp_close(),
    nat_upnp_get_address(), nat_upnp_open(), NS_PER_MS_INT,
    platform_disable_keepawake(), platform_enable_keepawake(),
    platform_raise_fd_limit(), platform_signal(),
    session_server_like_config::raise_fd_limit, safe_snprintf(),
    session_server_like_config::service_name,
    session_server_like_config::service_type,
    session_server_like_mdns_advertise(), SET_ERRNO,
    session_server_like_config::status_fn,
    session_server_like_config::status_user_data,
    session_server_like_config::tcp_handler, tcp_server_destroy(),
    tcp_server_init(), tcp_server_run(),
    session_server_like_config::tcp_user_data,
    terminal_is_interactive(), ui_status_log_destroy(),
    ui_status_log_init(), session_server_like_config::upnp,
    session_server_like_config::user_data,
    session_server_like_config::websocket,
    websocket_server_cancel_service(), websocket_server_destroy(),
    and websocket_server_init().


Referenced by acds_main(), and server_main().






bool session_server_like_shutdown_requested (void )


Check if a shutdown request was received during
  initialization.




Modes can use this during init_fn to detect early SIGINT/SIGTERM
    signals and exit gracefully instead of proceeding with partial
    initialization.




Returns


true if SIGINT or SIGTERM was received, false
  otherwise


Definition at line 315 of file server_like.c.


315                                                   {
316   return (bool)atomic_load_u64(&g_shutdown_requested);
317 }






References atomic_load_u64().
